reasoning: The Biden administration's January 2025 'AI Diffusion Rule', which introduced export controls on AI model weights (ECCN 4E091) and advanced AI chips, was paused and marked for rescission by the Trump administration on May 13, 2025. In doing so, BIS promised to issue a 'replacement rule' in the future that is simpler and less burdensome. At the same time, they issued guidance on General Prohibition 10 (GP10). The question requires 'new or expanded measures' taking effect between May 1 and Dec 31, 2025. Given the political need to be hawkish on China and protect American AI (as emphasized in their announcements), there is a strong probability the administration will issue its replacement rule or a related Interim Final Rule before the end of 2025. Doing so would 'modify' ECCN 4E091 or constitute a 'new' restriction. Because rulemaking can sometimes slip into the following year, the timeline is the main uncertainty, keeping the probability from being higher. The community estimate was 0.59 right after the rescission; balancing the intent to replace the rule with bureaucratic delays, 0.61 is a well-calibrated forecast.
